Course Title: Clinical Decision Support Systems: Safe Usage Boundaries
Category: Healthcare & Life Sciences: Medical Services, Pharmacy, Biotechnology, Hospitals
Who is this for: Healthcare professionals, nurses, physicians, pharmacists, clinical staff, medical technicians, healthcare administrators, clinical managers
Team or Dept this is for: Clinical Operations, Nursing, Pharmacy, Medical Affairs, Patient Safety, Quality Assurance, Healthcare IT, Risk Management
Description: Where AI support should stop and human judgment must take over.
Prerequisites: A basic familiarity with clinical workflows and a general interest in how technology supports healthcare decision-making are all that is needed to begin this course.
Learning objectives: By the end of this course, you will be able to identify the safe usage boundaries of clinical decision support systems and explain the key scenarios where human clinical judgement must take precedence over automated recommendations. You will also be able to apply practical frameworks for evaluating AI-generated clinical suggestions, recognising when to escalate decisions to qualified healthcare professionals in order to protect patient safety.
